steps.description: Click the "App features" link in the navigation menu on the left side of the "Apps: Developer Portal" dashboard interface.
Click the "Personal app" feature card's red plus (+) icon located under the "Select a feature to add" section in the "App features" page of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the "Create your first personal app tab" button in the Developer Portal interface under the "Personal app" section.

Type text: '${{var:app_name:vscuse_app_tdp#####}}' into the "Tab name" input field within the "Add a tab to your personal app" dialog box in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal application interface.
Click on the "Content URL" text field within the "Add a tab to your personal app" dialog box in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Type 'https://www.bing.com' into the 'Content URL' field within the 'Add a tab to your personal app' form on the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the "Scope" dropdown in the "Add a tab to your personal app" dialog within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the checkbox labeled "person" under the "Scope" dropdown in the "Add a tab to your personal app" configuration pane within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the checkbox labelled "groupChat" under the "Scope" section in the "Add a tab to your personal app" dialog in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the "Confirm" button in the "Add a tab to your personal app" form within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the "Confirm" button in the "Add a tab to your personal app" dialog within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the "Save" button in the bottom-right corner of the "Personal app" configuration page within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click on the "App features" menu option in the left navigation pane to access the app feature configuration section.
Press the "End" key to scroll to the bottom of the "App features" configuration section in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
@assertion 'Configure content URL(s) for debugging' is exist
Click the "+" button in the "Configure content URL(s) for debugging" dropdown at the top-right of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown menu in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select the project root folder location.
Press the 'Enter' key to confirm the input in the application name field shown in the top bar of the Visual Studio Code interface.
